justification: All five required pass criteria met: (1) Audit log gap clearly identified — 'omits the operation type, target user, and affected resource' with multiple detailed gaps listed. (2) PII-in-logs risk identified with specific compliance references (PCI DSS, GDPR). (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure. (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified. (5) Public internet exposure escalated to 'Coach K → user/infra team' rather than unilaterally mandated. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough identification of all five operational security gaps with specific compliance framework references (PCI DSS Requirement 3.4, GDPR Article 5(1)(c))
Public internet exposure correctly escalated as an infrastructure gap routed to 'Coach K → user/infra team' rather than Pippen unilaterally mandating a solution
RBAC gap appropriately escalated to security team rather than mandated by Pippen, showing correct understanding of authority boundaries
Stub /health endpoint identified as a lie that masks real dependency failures — goes beyond minimum rubric requirements
Strong 3am on-call framing throughout observability review demonstrates operational thinking
Identified PII purge requirement for existing logs — a remediation detail many reviews miss

Output is very verbose — some recommendations (RBAC details, specific infrastructure solutions) go beyond Pippen's integration/stability role into architecture territory, though they are appropriately escalated rather than mandated
